message:
test(proptest): add scan-pipeline + trigger-scanner + rule-ordering MRs

Extend `proptest_patterns_metamorphic.rs` past the high-level
`PatternEngine::detect` surface to cover the three layers beneath it
that have failed in the past but weren't pinned by the existing MRs:

1. **`TriggerScanner` byte-level append monotonicity.** The existing
   `arb_safe_suffix` strategy exercises string-level appends against
   `PatternEngine`, not the byte-level Aho-Corasick matcher. This MR
   builds a benign-digit-only suffix (0–9 — proven disjoint from all
   default trigger patterns, with a `prop_assume!` guard so the MR
   self-invalidates if the pattern set ever learns a digit-bearing
   trigger) and asserts that feeding `haystack` then `haystack || suffix`
   can never reduce the multiset of matches. The digit-alphabet choice
   deliberately avoids the circular oracle where "benign" would be
   defined by the very scanner under test.

2. **`ScanPipeline::process` vs `process_chunk + flush`.** The chunked
   API is what the live capture pipeline actually runs in production;
   the batch API is what most unit tests exercise. Aho-Corasick
   `LeftmostFirst` non-overlapping matching is context-dependent across
   chunk boundaries, so `flush()` does a batch rescan to close that
   gap. This MR randomly splits a byte input, drives both paths, and
   requires identical trigger counts — pinning that rescan as load-
   bearing so a future "optimization" that drops it fails loudly.

3. **`PatternEngine::detect` under rule permutation (disjoint anchors).**
   Uses three anchors chosen to be pairwise-disjoint substrings
   (`USAGE_LIMIT_REACHED`, `COMPACTION_COMPLETE_TAG`,
   `RATELIMIT_BLOCKED_SIG`) with compile-time-safe `prop_assert!`s
   confirming disjointness. Permuting the rule registration order
   across all 6 permutations of 3 must never change the fired
   `rule_id` set, because Aho-Corasick output for disjoint anchors is
   rule-order independent. Any bug that lets registration order leak
   through (e.g. HashMap iteration semantics, an unsorted-vec output,
   a tie-breaker keyed on insertion index) fails this MR.

Adds imports for `TriggerScanner`, `AgentType`/`PatternPack`/`RuleDef`/
`Severity`, and `ChunkedPipelineState`/`ScanPipeline`/`ScanPipelineConfig`
plus the shared `benign_digit_suffix`, `any_text_bytes`, `split_points`,
`slice_at_splits`, `chunked_trigger_counts`, `disjoint_rules`,
`engine_from`, and `detection_id_set` helpers that the new MRs share.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
